commands.review_summary:
This PR expands the code-mode `exec` tool and parameter descriptions to state module, language, and catalog-bridge constraints, plus an Unreleased changelog entry.

Reproducibility: no. not for the claimed live model turn savings. Source inspection shows current main omits constraints that the runtime and docs enforce, and the PR body has a before trace, but there is no patched-build after trace yet.
commands.review_followup: Contributor proof is the remaining gate; ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair because the code diff has no narrow correctness defect to fix.

target.title: fix(code-mode): sharpen exec tool description so models stop wasting turns rediscovering constraints
target.body:
## Problem

When OpenClaw code mode is enabled, the `exec` tool's description tells the model to use `tools.search` / `describe` / `call` to call enabled tools but does not state any of the runtime's actual constraints. In observed agent traces, the model burns turns rediscovering them:

- It tries `language: "bash"` because "code mode" reads as "any code"; gets rejected.
- It writes `require('fs')` (or another Node built-in) to do I/O; gets `"code mode module access is disabled."` and loops trying simpler constructs (`let x = 1`, bare expression) to figure out which patterns are allowed.
- It rediscovers, turn by turn, that Node modules are unavailable and that the catalog bridge is the only path to I/O.

## Change and value

Sharpen the description so the model sees the constraints up front:

- Node modules and `require` / `import` are NOT available.
- The `language` field accepts only `"javascript"` or `"typescript"`.
- The catalog bridge (`tools.search` → `tools.describe` → `tools.call`) is the path for any shell, file, network, or external action.

Parameter descriptions also state which symbols are in scope (`tools`, `ALL_TOOLS`) versus what is not (Node built-ins).

No runtime behavior changes — only the tool-card text the model sees.

## Who's affected

All agents with code mode enabled (globally or per-agent), regardless of provider. Most directly: models that don't already know the catalog-bridge contract from training-set examples — typically smaller open-weight models routed through the Pi-embedded-runner.

## Why now

Per-agent code mode shipped in 2026.5.18 (#83473). A controlled re-enable for evaluation surfaced multiple sessions where the agent spent 6+ turns probing the same constraints before stabilizing on the bridge pattern. Description-only fixes are the cheapest path to reduce that floor; the rest of the friction is structural and will need separate work.

## Implementation

`src/agents/code-mode.ts` — `createCodeModeTools`:
- `exec` tool `description` expanded with the three constraints.
- `code` parameter description states scope explicitly (`tools` and `ALL_TOOLS` in scope; Node built-ins not).
- `language` parameter description states the enum.

`CHANGELOG.md` — entry under `## Unreleased / ### Changes`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When code mode is enabled, models routed through the Pi-embedded-runner waste turns rediscovering that `language: "bash"` is rejected, that `require` / `import` is unavailable, and that the `tools.search` / `describe` / `call` bridge is the path to I/O. The current `exec` description does not state any of those constraints.

- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (commit `50a2481`); agent routed to `ollama-cloud/kimi-k2.6` via the Pi-embedded-runner; `tools.codeMode.enabled: true`. Trace captured from a real session — not a unit-test mock.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # Confirm patched description string is present in the source file:
  grep -A6 'CODE_MODE_EXEC_TOOL_NAME' src/agents/code-mode.ts | head -8

  # Confirm no test snapshot or other source references the old description string:
  grep -rln 'Use ALL_TOOLS and tools.search/describe/call' test/ docs/ src/
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Observed loop in a real agent session BEFORE this patch:
  ```
  L9   toolResult: {"status":"failed","error":"code mode module access is disabled."}
  L12  thinking: 'The `language` parameter only allows specific values - "bash" is not one of them.'
  L17  thinking: 'The `exec` tool now strictly requires `code` and only accepts "javascript" as `language`.'
  L22  toolResult: code mode module access is disabled
  L24  toolResult: code mode module access is disabled
  L26  toolResult: code mode module access is disabled
  L29  thinking: '`let x=1;x` worked! The issue with the fs calls is "code mode module access is disabled."'
  L31  thinking: 'Even `const fs=require("fs")` triggers "code mode module access is disabled."'
  ```
  Each entry above is the model paying for a separate turn to rediscover a constraint that the description does not state. After this patch the description names all three constraints (`require`/`import` unavailable, `language` enum, bridge pattern) directly. Repository grep (above) confirms no snapshot or other source carries the old string, so the edit is contained.

- **Observed result after fix**: The model sees the constraints in the tool-card text before its first attempt. The expected behavior change is that agents stop emitting `language: "bash"` and stop probing `require()` to discover module-access denial. The description is now self-contained: a model reading it does not need to run a trial to learn what is allowed.

- **What was not tested**: A live before-vs-after dispatch of the same prompt against a patched build — this PR is intentionally in draft to flag that the after-trace will be added as a follow-up comment once a patched build is dispatched against the same probe set. The change has no runtime path (it edits two description string literals plus an unreleased CHANGELOG line), but a controlled empirical confirmation that the new wording reduces per-turn constraint-rediscovery is the natural next step.
target.author: kaspre
